Section 1  
Create a Python function named `count_word_occurrences` that takes two parameters: `text` and `word`. The function should return the number of times the `word` appears in the `text`, ignoring case sensitivity.

Section 2  
Use the `lower()` method to convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison. Split the `text` into words using the `split()` method and iterate through each word to check if it matches the lowercase version of the `word`.

Section 3  
Initialize a counter variable to keep track of the occurrences. Loop through each word in the split list and compare it to the lowercase `word`. If they match, increment the counter. Finally, return the counter value.

Section 4  
Test the function with the provided example to ensure it returns the correct count. Print the result and confirm that the function adheres to the requirements of being case-insensitive and counting all occurrences of the word in the text.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. The final response starts and ends with the word "response". The phrase "The pen is mightier than sword" must be repeated exactly twice. All lowercase words appear at most three times. The response is under 36 sentences long.
